query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "omniretrieval: unified retrieval across heterogeneous knowledge source" linguistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (b) by measuring the performance and extensibility of multilingual language models (the dependent variable) when adapting to low-resource settings via vocabulary augmentation and transliteration (mechanisms relevant to extending retrieval capabilities across heterogeneous linguistic sources). It also aligns with criterion (f) as it addresses the canonical alternative-vocabulary cluster for "extending LLMs linguistically" through the lens of low-resource adaptation
